payload.text: Since the last digest (evt-2026-07-22-digest-2), thirteen log entries tested every remaining lever the research campaign had flagged as open, closing four axes in one day. A 50-single-name-stock universe was pinned (~4-5 per GICS sector, split-adjusted, 2263 sessions, zero truncation) to test whether the existing 16-symbol snapshot's PC1-dominated diversity problem (71.7% of variance) was a universe-size issue: cross-sectional-momentum-stocks50 (sector-rotation's mechanism, reused unmodified) scored Sharpe 0.280 -- essentially unchanged from sector-rotation's 0.255 on 10 sector ETFs, with ~5x the turnover and the vault's first OOS-degrading result (-44.2%) -- closing the universe-scale lever: universe size was not the limiting factor for this mechanism. Three genuinely new, non-momentum mechanism classes were then tried for the first time. Low-vol-anomaly-stocks50 (rank by trailing realized vol, hold the least-volatile 15 of 50) posted this vault's best cross-sectional result yet (Sharpe 0.519) but the anomaly INVERTED rather than merely failed to appear -- volatility fell 14% while return fell 31%, most likely because this sample's persistent 2016-2024 long bias structurally penalizes defensive tilts. Pairs-trading-stocks50 (the vault's first long-short, market-neutral strategy -- required extending risk.py/signal.py to support short positions, verified backward-compatible at 202/202 tests) screened same-sector pairs via Engle-Granger cointegration and traded the three strongest (JNJ/ABT, CVX/COP, DUK/SO, all p<0.05 full-sample), but scored this vault's first NEGATIVE Sharpe (-0.540): only 18-24% of trades converged, and none of the three pairs' cointegration held up when re-tested on the second half of the same sample alone (p=0.25-0.33) -- the full-sample screen likely picked up shared bull-market drift rather than genuine mean-reversion, banking a reusable methodological lesson that stability-across-sub-periods belongs in the screening step itself, not a post-hoc check. The overnight-return effect (close-to-open vs. open-to-close decomposition on SPY/QQQ) replicated directionally -- overnight Sharpe clears intraday on both symbols (0.675/0.842 vs. 0.440/0.485) -- but turned out un-backtestable as a registered strategy at all, since the engine prices everything off close only with no intra-session split; computed directly from the snapshot's own open/close columns instead, it proved structurally uneconomic at this vault's standard 20bps/session cost (net Sharpe -3.7/-2.9), a cost-structure mismatch rather than a falsification of the underlying effect. A closing postmortem synthesizes all four: each failed for a different structural reason (inverted premise, spurious cointegration, cost-structure mismatch, no universe-size effect), and every mechanism-design and universe-design lever this campaign and its two prior digests raised has now been tested at least once. Open question for the next research pass: keep searching mechanism space (options-implied signals, a genuinely different data source) or pause backtest-only research on these same fixed 2016-2024/2021-2024 windows -- the vault-best result (tsmom-ms-shift-tltgld-blend, Sharpe 0.9896, still the closest anything has come to the standing 1.0 gate) remains unbeaten by all four of today's new attempts, and further recombination on identical fixed samples risks tuning against them rather than finding real edge.
